title: Fix RBAC: Principal-based admin authorization for user management

requirements.text: Implement industry-standard RBAC in the backend: the first principal to register is automatically assigned the admin role and stored in stable state. All subsequent admin authorization checks must compare the caller's Internet Identity principal against the stored admin principal list. Remove or replace any hardcoded principal or password-based admin checks that currently cause the 'Unauthorized: Only admins can create users' error.

quotes: Unauthorized: Only admins can create users
Principal-based authentication — admin checks are done by comparing the caller's Internet Identity principal against the stored admin principal(s).

acceptanceCriteria: The first Internet Identity principal to call a registration or initialization function is stored as the admin in stable state.
All backend functions that require admin privileges (e.g., createUser, deleteUser, updateUser) check caller principal against the stored admin principal(s).
A signed-in admin principal can successfully create, edit, and delete managed users without receiving an 'Unauthorized' error.
Non-admin principals are correctly rejected with an authorization error when attempting admin-only operations.
No hardcoded principal IDs or password-based admin checks remain in the authorization flow.

requirements.text: Update all frontend API calls in SettingsPage.tsx, CreateUserDialog.tsx, EditUserDialog.tsx, and any related hooks in useQueries.ts to pass the authenticated Internet Identity principal through the actor when invoking user management backend functions. Ensure the actor used for these calls is the authenticated actor (not anonymous) so the backend receives the correct caller principal.

quotes: Unauthorized: Only admins can create users
the Internet Identity principal must be correctly passed through the actor/agent setup and recognized by backend authorization checks

acceptanceCriteria: All user management mutations (create, edit, delete user) in useQueries.ts use the authenticated actor from useActor hook.
The actor passed to SettingsPage and user management dialogs is the authenticated actor tied to the current Internet Identity session.
After signing in as admin, submitting the Create User form succeeds without an 'Unauthorized' error.
The actor is not the anonymous actor when admin-protected backend calls are made.

requirements.text: Implement a backend function that allows the current admin to grant admin role to other registered principals, and another to revoke admin role. Expose these in the frontend SettingsPage under user management so admins can promote or demote users.

quotes: Admin can grant/revoke roles — admins can promote other users to admin or create users with specific roles.

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a grantAdmin(principal) function callable only by existing admins.
Backend exposes a revokeAdmin(principal) function callable only by existing admins.
SettingsPage displays role management controls for existing users visible only to admins.
Granting or revoking admin role is reflected immediately in the user list.

requirements.text: Add a backend query function that returns the current caller's role and admin status based on their Internet Identity principal. Use this in the frontend AuthContext or Header to correctly display the admin role badge and control access to admin-only UI sections without relying solely on locally stored session data.

quotes: Is there a way in the app to view or verify which principal is currently recognized as admin
Principal-based authentication — admin checks are done by comparing the caller's Internet Identity principal

acceptanceCriteria: Backend exposes a getMyRole() query that returns the caller's role based on their principal.
Frontend calls getMyRole() after login to verify the authenticated user's actual role from the backend.
The admin role badge in the Header reflects the principal-verified role.
Admin-only navigation items (e.g., Settings in Sidebar) are shown/hidden based on the principal-verified role.

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file.
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Admin role assignment must be dynamic (stored in stable state) — no hardcoded principal IDs.
Generate migration.mo to preserve existing stable state when upgrading backend state schema.
nonGoals: Changing the visual design or theme of the application.
Adding new asset, repair ticket, or parts inventory features.
Implementing OAuth or third-party authentication providers.
Replacing Internet Identity with any other authentication mechanism.
